section.data-narration: Local Connectedness is a weaker form of connectedness.
section.text: A point x in a metric space (X, d) is said to be locally connected at x if for every open set U containing x, there exists an open neighborhood V of x such that V ⊆ U.

section.data-narration: A space is locally connected if every point is a local minimum of the distance function d(x, \cdot).
section.text: - A space is locally connected if every point is a local minimum of the distance function d(x, \cdot).
- The open mapping theorem states that a continuous function from a locally connected space to a locally connected space is open.

section.data-narration: Arc-Wise Connectedness is a stronger form of connectedness.
section.text: A subset A of a metric space (X, d) is said to be arc-wise connected if for every two points x, y ∈ A, there exists a continuous function f: [0, 1] → A such that f(0) = x and f(1) = y.

section.data-narration: Arc-Wise Connectedness is a stronger form of connectedness.
section.text: Consider the space ℝ with the standard metric. The point x = 0 is arc-wise connected because for every two points x, y ∈ ℝ, there exists a continuous function f: [0, 1] → ℝ such that f(0) = x and f(1) = y.

section.data-narration: Key Concepts for Arc-Wise Connectedness include:
section.text: - The open mapping theorem states that a continuous function from a locally connected space to a locally connected space is open.
